_meta.description: Search vocabulary for each node in the No-Man causal graph. Used by src/literature.py to construct Semantic Scholar queries. This is metadata — not scientific record.

schema.node_id: Must match an id in knowledge/nodes.json
schema.terms_en: 2-4 academic search terms in English. Use real academic vocabulary, not node ID strings.
schema.domain_context: List of context qualifiers always included in queries. Always includes 'regional bank' and '地方銀行' or 'chiho ginko'.
schema.notes: Usage notes for query construction; disambiguation guidance

vocabulary.notes: Prefer 'bank lending' or 'credit supply' over 'loan volume'. In Japanese banking literature, 'bank credit channel' and 'credit crunch' are the most-cited terms for this mechanism. Avoid 'bank loans outstanding' (balance-sheet focus) when the causal mechanism is credit availability.

Use 'bank capital adequacy ratio' in academic searches. 'Tier 1 capital ratio' is the Basel III variant. In Japanese banking crisis literature, 'capital crunch' and 'capital constraint' are the mechanism-level terms. The FSA domestic standard is 4%; BIS international standard is 8%.

NIM is the standard academic abbreviation. In Japanese regional banking literature, the related term '預貸金利鞘' (yotai kinri-zaya, deposit-loan spread) is commonly used. Under ZIRP/NIRP context, search for 'NIM compression' and 'low interest rate environment bank profitability'.

NPL is the universal academic abbreviation. In Japanese banking literature, '不良債権' (furyou saiken) is the standard term covering both risk-managed loans (要管理債権) and bankrupt/near-bankrupt categories. The 1990s-2000s banking crisis literature (Hoshi, Kashyap, Peek, Rosengren) uses 'problem loans' interchangeably. FSA classification is stricter than international Basel standards.

Use 'non-interest income' as the primary academic term. In Japanese banking context, 役務取引等収益 covers fund distribution (投資信託販売), insurance agency (保険代理), and settlement services. Search specifically for 'bank revenue diversification Japan' and 'relationship lending cross-selling' for mechanism papers.

OHR (overhead ratio) and cost-to-income ratio are used interchangeably in academic literature. FSA uses OHR as a key sustainability indicator. Search for 'bank efficiency ratio Japan' and 'X-efficiency regional banks'. In Japanese context, high OHR is often associated with excessive branch networks in depopulating areas.

In Japanese regional banking, 'deposit base' reflects both funding capacity and community financial engagement. Search for 'bank deposit stability', 'retail deposit outflows', and 'bank liquidity Japan'. The structural demographic link is best found under 'population ageing bank deposits Japan'.

terms_ja: 有価証券ポートフォリオ
vocabulary.notes: JGB (Japanese Government Bond) is the dominant term in Bank of Japan and IMF papers. The Japanese term 国債 (kokuai) refers specifically to JGBs while 地方債 (chihosai) refers to local government bonds. Both are critical for Japanese regional banks. Under rate normalization context, search for 'bank duration risk Japan' and 'unrealized bond losses Japan banks'.

SME (small and medium enterprise) is standard in English. Japanese equivalents: 中小企業 (chusho kigyo). 'Credit access' and 'credit availability' are preferred over 'credit demand' when the research question concerns supply-side constraints. 'Credit rationing' literature is central. Tsuruta 2014 and Ogawa/Suzuki 1998 are key Japan-specific citations.

In regional banking impact papers, employment is typically the downstream welfare variable reached through credit → investment → employment chains. Search for 'bank credit regional employment Japan' and 'credit crunch employment Japan'. BoJ regional economic surveys (地域経済報告) are primary data sources.

In Japan, the prefectural-level measure is '県内総生産' (ken-nai soso seisan, gross prefectural product). National accounts use 'GDP'; prefecture-level studies use 'GPP' or 'gross regional product'. Combine with 'bank credit' for mechanism papers: 'bank lending regional GDP Japan'.

In Japanese literature, '設備投資' (setsubi toshi, equipment/facilities investment) is the standard term. 'Capital expenditure' and 'fixed investment' are used interchangeably in English academic papers. Ogawa & Suzuki 1998 and Tsuruta 2014 are the central Japan-specific citations for the credit → investment link.

In Japan, the housing starts (住宅着工戸数) data from MLIT is the primary proxy. 'Real estate lending' is more relevant than 'housing market' for the bank → housing channel. Under population decline context, add 'depopulation housing Japan' as a modifier. The collateral value channel is important: 'land prices Japan bank lending'.

Japanese local government bonds are 地方債 (chiho-sai). The fiscal health metric in Japan is often measured by the 財政力指数 (fiscal capacity index) or 実質公債費比率 (actual debt service ratio). For the bank-local government nexus, search for 'regional bank local government bond Japan' and 'bank municipal bond holdings'.

The 'main bank' concept is a Japan-specific institution extensively documented in Hoshi, Kashyap, Scharfstein (1990s). Search for 'main bank Japan', 'keiretsu bank', and 'delegated monitoring Japan'. In regional banking, 'relationship lending' and 'soft information lending' are the international equivalents. Uchida et al. 2008 is the key regional bank citation.

The academic concept linking branches to lending quality is 'soft information' and 'relationship lending'. Search for 'bank branch relationship lending', 'geographic proximity bank SME', and 'branch closure credit access'. In Japanese context, search specifically for '支店閉鎖 中小企業 融資' or 'Japan branch reduction SME credit'.

Market structure is typically measured by Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI) or concentration ratios (CR3, CR5). The competition-stability tradeoff literature is relevant (Petersen & Rajan 1995 on competition and relationship lending). Ogura 2010 is the key Japan regional bank citation. Search for 'bank competition SME lending Japan' and 'regional bank market power'.

terms_ja: コーポレートガバナンス
vocabulary.notes: Board independence is a key measurable proxy. FSA launched governance reform guidelines (コーポレートガバナンス・コード) post-2015. Search for 'bank board independence performance Japan', 'bank governance NPL Japan', and 'insider board Japanese bank'. IMF 2017 and Berger et al. 2005 are the international reference papers cited in seed_graph.json.

The primary empirical proxy is BoJ Tankan survey data (日銀短観), specifically the SME component. Search for 'Tankan business confidence Japan bank' and 'SME business sentiment bank relationship Japan'. Ogawa & Suzuki 1998 is the key citation for the credit-confidence-investment channel.

Bank run literature uses terms like 'bank run', 'deposit flight', 'panic withdrawal'. In Japan, the failure of Hogo and Tokuyo banks in the late 1990s provides empirical case studies. Japan introduced full deposit insurance in 1996 (expanded to full payoff guarantee post-1996 crisis). Search for 'Japan deposit insurance bank run', 'regional bank stability depositor', and 'bank reputation deposit outflow'.

New firm formation rate (新規開業率) is the standard empirical proxy in Japanese data (from the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare business statistics). Search for 'bank credit entrepreneurship Japan', 'credit availability startup formation', and 'regional bank new business Japan'. Ogura 2010 covers the competition-credit-entrepreneurship channel.

Bank reputation in regional contexts is often proxied by deposit market share, media sentiment, or survey-based trust indices. In the academic literature, 'bank reputation' is most studied in crisis contexts (bank failures, NPL disclosures). Search for 'bank reputation NPL disclosure', 'regional bank community trust Japan', and 'signaling bank distress reputation'. The E-045 speculative edge targets this node.

FSA (金融庁, Financial Services Agency) supervises regional banks in Japan. '行政指導' (gyosei shido, administrative guidance) is the informal supervisory channel unique to the Japanese regulatory system. Search for 'FSA bank examination Japan', 'bank supervisory pressure Japan', and 'moral suasion bank regulation Japan'. Watanabe 2007 is the key citation for supervisory-capital interaction.

Distinguish 'regulatory capital pressure' (FSA-driven, mechanism) from 'capital ratio' (the bank's own metric). Search for 'supervisory pressure bank capital Japan', 'FSA prompt corrective action precautionary', and 'bank deleveraging supervisory pressure Japan'. Hoshi & Kashyap 2001 and Watanabe 2007 cover the supervisory-capital-lending transmission.

terms_ja: バーゼル規制遵守コスト
vocabulary.notes: Basel III/IV implementation costs are disproportionately large for smaller regional banks. Search for 'Basel III compliance cost small bank', 'regulatory burden community bank', and 'proportionality banking regulation'. The BCBS 2019 evaluation and EBA compliance cost studies are referenced in seed_graph.json (E-018). In Japanese context, search for 'バーゼルIII 地域銀行 コスト' or 'Basel proportionality Japan regional bank'.

Japan's Prompt Corrective Action (早期是正措置) system was introduced in 1996-1998. PCA triggers are tied to capital ratio thresholds. Search for 'Japan prompt corrective action bank', 'PCA capital threshold bank lending', and 'bank failure prevention Japan supervision'. Hoshi & Kashyap 2001 provides historical context for PCA introduction. The anticipatory deleveraging mechanism is key.

Japan's demographic decline is one of the most acute globally. '過疎化' (kasoka, depopulation) specifically refers to rural outmigration. Search for 'Japan regional population decline bank', 'aging population regional bank Japan', and 'demographic decline local economy'. In banking contexts, combine with 'deposit base', 'SME survival', and 'loan demand decline'.

The policy rate instrument is the uncollateralized overnight call rate (無担保コール翌日物レート). Under ZIRP/NIRP (2010-2024), policy rate changes had limited NIM transmission. Since 2024, rate normalization is underway. Search for 'Bank of Japan interest rate regional bank profitability', 'BoJ rate normalization NIM', and 'ZIRP NIRP bank lending Japan'. The interest rate regime matters critically for sign interpretation.
